home.meta_title: Fuel Economy Calculator - Save on Fuel | FuelEcon Canada
home.meta_description: Calculate fuel costs in CAD, compare vehicle efficiency, and find fuel-saving tips for Canadian drivers.
home.ev_notice_html: ⚡ Electric vehicle? Compare EV vs Gas savings

faq.q: How does FuelEcon calculate trip costs?
faq.a: We take your vehicle's MPG, the total distance of your trip in kilometres, and the current gas price to work out exactly how many litres you'll need and what they will cost.

Your car has a base MPG, but things like low tire pressure or driving on back roads instead of the highway make it less efficient. Effective MPG is what your car is actually getting on that specific drive.

They are very close for most drivers! We use official data for vehicle averages and live gas prices. However, things like heavy cargo, aggressive driving, or very cold weather can change your real results.

faq.q: Why does tire pressure matter for gas?
faq.a: Low tires create more 'rolling resistance,' meaning your engine has to work harder to move the car. Keeping them inflated to the recommended level can save you about 3% to 10% on gas.

faq.q: Is it better to drive with windows down or A/C on?
faq.a: Below 80 km/h, windows down is usually better. At highway speeds, the 'drag' from open windows actually costs more gas than running the A/C.

faq.q: How much can I save by driving slower?
faq.a: For most cars, gas mileage drops significantly above 100 km/h. Every 10 km/h you drive over 100 is like paying an extra 5-10 cents per litre.

faq.q: Does idling use a lot of gas?
faq.a: Yes. If you are stopped for more than 30 seconds (and not in traffic), it's usually cheaper to turn the engine off and restart it than to let it idle.
On flat highways, yes. It helps maintain a steady speed, which is much more efficient than constant micro-adjustments with your foot.

faq.q: Why do roof racks affect fuel economy?
faq.a: They break the aerodynamic shape of your car. Even an empty roof rack can reduce your MPG by 2% to 5% because of wind resistance.
The Green Zone is our way of showing you're driving at peak efficiency. When your route and tires are optimized, you're in the Green Zone!

faq.q: Can I save my car for later?
faq.a: Yes! Use the 'Save this car' button to keep your vehicle's info in your browser so you don't have to type it in next time.

faq.q: Where do you get gas prices?
faq.a: We use weekly averages from official Canadian sources like Natural Resources Canada.

tips: 🌿 Below 50 mph, crack windows instead of A/C — A/C can cost 3-4 MPG.
🛣️ Steady 55-65 mph on the highway is the MPG sweet spot for most cars.
📦 Roof racks tank fuel economy — take them off when you're not using them.
⛽ Cruise control on flat highway = 7-14% MPG improvement.
🔧 A clogged air filter can cost you 10% MPG. Change it when dirty.
🚗 Combine errands — cold starts use 2x more fuel per mile than warm driving.
🕐 Idling over 30 seconds burns more gas than restarting the engine.
🏔️ Drive down hills in gear, not neutral — modern cars cut fuel when engine-braking.

ecoAdvice.highwaySaving: 🛣️ Take the highway if you can — you'd save about CA${savings} on this exact trip.
ecoAdvice.highwayOk: ✅ Highway route — already the cheapest option for fuel.
ecoAdvice.tiresLow: ⚠️ Your tires are low. Inflating them is free and gives you back about 8% MPG.
ecoAdvice.acceleration: 📉 Smooth acceleration = 10-30% less fuel over a tank.

evVsGas.title: EV vs Gas Break-Even Calculator
evVsGas.description: Find out when an electric vehicle pays for itself compared to a gas car.
evVsGas.heading: Is an EV Worth It?
evVsGas.result_never: EV never pays back under these assumptions
evVsGas.result_explanation: At the break-even point, fuel savings equal the EV's higher upfront cost.
